name: Frozen Cranberry Souffle with Spun Sugar Cran
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the cranberry mixture: In a heavy saucepan, combine cranberries, sugar, and water. Bring to a boil and stir until sugar is dissolved. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes or until thickened. Let cool completely.
2. Make the Italian Meringue: In a small heavy saucepan, combine sugar and water. Bring to a boil and stir until sugar is dissolved. Boil syrup, washing down any crystals with cold water, until it reaches 248°F on a candy thermometer. Remove from heat. Beat egg whites with salt until holding soft peaks. Add hot syrup in a stream while beating until meringue cools to room temperature.
3. Gently fold the cooled cranberry mixture into the Italian meringue. In another bowl, beat cream until it holds stiff peaks and gently fold into the cranberry mixture.
4. Spoon the soufflé into a 2.5-quart freezer-safe glass serving bowl (8-inch diameter), smoothing the top, and cover with plastic wrap. Freeze overnight. The soufflé can be made up to 3 days in advance and kept frozen.
5. Make the spun sugar wreath: In a small heavy saucepan, combine corn syrup and sugar. Bring to a boil over moderate heat until dissolved. Boil until golden caramel and registers 320°F on a candy thermometer. Remove from heat and let cool for 30 seconds. Drizzle syrup over cranberries in a 6-inch wide wreath shape. Repeat until covered. If thick, reheat until proper consistency.
6. Cool wreath completely. Garnish the soufflé with the wreath and mint sprigs before serving.
(Note: The soufflé will be very hard when frozen. Remove from freezer about an hour and a half before serving to allow it to thaw slightly. It remains safe to serve even if partially thawed.)
